title: Energy Catalyst Round 11 – Early Stage: Up to £300,000 Funding for Clean Energy Innovations in Developing Countries (Apply by March 25, 2026)
summary: Innovators developing solutions to expand clean energy access in developing regions now have an opportunity to secure significant funding. Applications are open for Energy Catalyst Round 11 Early Stage , a funding competition supporting early-stage clean energy innovations in emerging markets. The initiative is delivered by Innovate UK and supported through the International Science Partnerships Fund . The program aims to accelerate innovative solutions that improve affordable and reliable energy access in developing economies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); With a total funding pool of up to 7 million , the program provides grant funding for early-stage projects that can contribute to sustainable and inclusive energy transitions across multiple regions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About Energy Catalyst Round 11 Energy Catalyst supports businesses and organizations developing technologies and business models that expand energy access while supporting climate goals. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); This round focuses on early-stage projects that demonstrate innovation and the potential to scale in underserved markets. Projects should focus on delivering clean, reliable, and affordable energy solutions for communities that currently lack adequate access to electricity. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Funding Details Selected projects will receive grant funding to support development and testing of innovative energy solutions. Funding parameters include: Total available funding: Up to 7 million Grant size per project: 50,000 300,000 Project stage: Early-stage innovation Funding type: Grant support for eligible project costs ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The competition is highly competitive, with previous rounds receiving many high-quality proposals. Eligible Regions Projects must focus on Official Development Assistance (ODA)-eligible countries located in the following regions: Sub-Saharan Africa South Asia Indo-Pacific Latin America Applicants should demonstrate how their innovation will address energy access challenges in these markets. What Innovate UK Is Looking For The program seeks projects that: Expand or improve energy access in underserved communities Support a just and inclusive energy transition Contribute to global climate and sustainability targets Successful projects will typically align with global sustainability priorities, including: United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 7 (Affordable and Clean Energy) Sustainable Development Goal 13 (Climate Action) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Innovations may include technologies, services, or business models that improve access to sustainable energy. Application Deadline Applications must be submitted by March 25, 2026 at 11:00 AM (UK time) . Applicants are encouraged to start early to ensure adequate preparation and submission before the deadline. How to Apply Learn more and submit your application through the official page. For more Grant Opportunities from OFY, Click Here. The post Energy Catalyst Round 11 – Early Stage: Up to £300,000 Funding for Clean Energy Innovations in Developing Countries (Apply by March 25, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship 2026: Equity-Free Funding for African EdTech Startups
summary: The Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship 2026 , delivered in partnership with Co-creation HUB (CcHUB) , is now accepting applications for its fourth cohort. This highly impactful program is designed to empower African entrepreneurs who are building innovative education technology solutions aimed at improving learning outcomes, expanding access to quality education, and supporting teachers and learners across the continent. As education systems around the world continue to evolve through technology, the need for scalable and impactful EdTech solutions has never been greater. In Africa, where millions of young people are seeking access to quality education and digital learning tools, innovative startups play a critical role in bridging gaps in accessibility, engagement, and learning outcomes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship addresses this need by supporting promising African startups with equity-free funding, expert mentorship, and a robust innovation ecosystem. The program aims to nurture the next generation of African EdTech leaders who are building transformative solutions for the continent s education sector. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); A Powerful Partnership Driving Educational Innovation in Africa The EdTech Fellowship is powered by a strategic collaboration between CcHUB , Africa s largest technology innovation center, and the Mastercard Foundation , one of the world s largest philanthropic organizations. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); CcHUB has built a reputation as a leading innovation hub in Africa, supporting technology-driven solutions that address social and economic challenges. Since its founding in 2010 as Nigeria s first innovation center, the organization has expanded across multiple African countries, including Nigeria, Rwanda, Kenya, and Namibia. Its programs have supported innovators and entrepreneurs in more than 40 countries across the continent. The Mastercard Foundation, established in 2006, is a Canadian charitable organization focused on advancing education and financial inclusion. Through partnerships with visionary institutions and innovators, the foundation supports initiatives that help young people in Africa and Indigenous youth in Canada access dignified and meaningful work. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Together, these two organizations have created a fellowship program that supports African startups building scalable EdTech solutions capable of transforming education systems. Program Impact and Achievements The Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship has already generated significant impact across Africa by supporting innovative startups and helping them scale their solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The program s growing network of entrepreneurs and innovators has collectively reached impressive milestones. Startups supported by the fellowship have acquired more than one million learners across various platforms, demonstrating the scalability of digital learning solutions. In addition, more than eighty thousand educators have benefited from tools and platforms designed to enhance teaching effectiveness. Educational institutions across Africa are increasingly adopting these solutions, with more than two thousand schools and learning organizations integrating technologies developed by fellowship-supported startups. These ventures have also successfully attracted follow-on investments, raising more than two million dollars in additional funding to expand their impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); These results demonstrate how targeted support for EdTech innovators can lead to transformative improvements in education access, quality, and engagement. What the Fellowship Offers Selected startups will receive comprehensive support designed to help them grow, refine their products, and scale their impact across Africa. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Product Development Support Startups receive guidance on aligning their technology solutions with real-world educational needs. Through expert mentorship and program workshops, founders refine their platforms to ensure they effectively address challenges faced by students, teachers, and institutions. Distribution and Market Expansion One of the biggest challenges for EdTech startups is reaching learners at scale. The fellowship helps startups develop effective distribution strategies that allow them to reach wider audiences and build sustainable user bases across different African markets. Funding and Investment Readiness Participants receive support in strengthening their business models and developing compelling narratives that attract investors. By improving financial metrics and investment readiness, startups become better positioned to secure follow-on funding after completing the program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Talent and Organizational Development The fellowship also supports founders in building resilient and ethical organizations. This includes expert guidance on human resource management, safeguarding policies, and leadership development to ensure that startups can scale responsibly and sustainably. Types of EdTech Startups the Fellowship Supports The program specifically targets startups that are developing innovative solutions addressing key challenges within the education ecosystem. Startups that expand access to quality education are strongly encouraged to apply. This includes solutions that help underserved communities access learning opportunities through digital platforms, mobile technology, or hybrid education models. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); The fellowship also supports tools that enhance teacher effectiveness and training. Solutions that empower educators with better teaching resources, analytics, and classroom management tools are highly valued. Innovations that increase learner engagement through interactive platforms, gamified learning, artificial intelligence, and personalized education pathways are another priority area. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Finally, the program looks for startups capable of generating scalable and sustainable impact in learning outcomes. Solutions that can expand across multiple regions or countries while maintaining quality and effectiveness are particularly attractive. A Thriving Community of EdTech Innovators Beyond financial and technical support, the fellowship offers access to a vibrant network of entrepreneurs, mentors, investors, and education experts. Participants become part of a growing community of African EdTech leaders who collaborate, share insights, and support one another in building impactful solutions. This community-driven approach fosters collaboration and accelerates innovation across the continent. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Several alumni of the program have achieved remarkable success after completing the fellowship. For example, the EdTech company Lingawa successfully closed a 1.1 million dollar pre-seed funding round and strengthened its internal organizational processes with the support of fellowship advisors. These success stories illustrate the program s ability to transform early-stage startups into high-impact ventures capable of reaching large numbers of learners. How the Fellowship is Shaping the Future of Learning in Africa Africa s rapidly growing youth population presents both an opportunity and a challenge for education systems. By supporting innovative entrepreneurs, the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship helps ensure that education technology can meet the needs of future generations. Through its combination of funding, mentorship, and ecosystem support, the program accelerates the development of solutions that redefine how learning happens across Africa. From digital classrooms and mobile learning platforms to teacher training tools and personalized learning systems, the fellowship is helping startups create solutions that can transform education for millions of learners. How to Apply Interested startups can apply to join Cohort IV of the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship through the official application platform provided by CcHUB. Applicants should ensure that their startups align with the fellowship s mission of improving learning outcomes, expanding access to quality education, and delivering scalable technological solutions within the African education sector. Entrepreneurs are encouraged to prepare strong applications that clearly demonstrate the impact, scalability, and innovation of their solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(138); }); Application Deadline The deadline for submitting applications for the Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship Cohort IV will be announced on the official CcHUB platform. Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible to ensure their submissions are fully reviewed. For more information and to submit your application click here Discover more opportunities here The post Mastercard Foundation EdTech Fellowship 2026: Equity-Free Funding for African EdTech Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 7 Doctoral Positions at the University of Konstanz: Join the “Politics of Inequality” Research Cluster in Germany
summary: The University of Konstanz in Germany is inviting applications for seven doctoral positions within the prestigious Cluster of Excellence titled The Politics of Inequality. This research initiative brings together leading scholars and early-career researchers who aim to better understand the political causes and consequences of inequality across societies. The program provides a unique opportunity for highly motivated graduates with strong academic backgrounds to pursue advanced doctoral research in an interdisciplinary environment. This opportunity is designed for graduates who wish to contribute to innovative research that examines inequality from multiple perspectives. Doctoral candidates will work closely with internationally recognized scholars while developing independent research projects or collaborating within established research teams. The positions are embedded in a vibrant academic community that focuses on exploring how inequality influences political systems, social structures, and collective action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Understanding the Research Focus: The Politics of Inequality Inequality remains one of the most pressing challenges faced by societies around the world. Differences in access to education, income distribution, wealth accumulation, environmental resources, and political rights continue to shape opportunities and outcomes for individuals and communities. The Cluster of Excellence The Politics of Inequality was created to examine these complex dynamics and to better understand how inequality emerges, how it is perceived by individuals and groups, and how it influences political decision-making and social mobilization. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Researchers in the cluster explore several key questions. They examine how people perceive inequalities in their societies and how these perceptions influence their attitudes toward fairness, redistribution, and social justice. The cluster also studies how these perceptions can lead to collective mobilization, social movements, and political participation. In addition, scholars investigate how political institutions, policymakers, and other actors respond to inequalities and how these responses shape public policy and governance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The cluster adopts a broad and multidimensional understanding of inequality . Rather than focusing only on economic disparities, it also considers inequalities related to education, environmental conditions, political rights, social privileges, and opportunities for participation in public life. This comprehensive perspective allows researchers to analyze how different forms of inequality interact with each other and influence political outcomes. An Interdisciplinary Academic Environment One of the most distinctive aspects of the Cluster of Excellence is its interdisciplinary approach. Addressing complex societal issues such as inequality requires insights from multiple academic disciplines. The cluster therefore brings together researchers from fields including political science, sociology, economics, linguistics, and other related social sciences. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By combining different theoretical frameworks and research methodologies, the cluster creates a dynamic environment where doctoral candidates can learn from diverse perspectives. Collaboration across disciplines allows researchers to develop more comprehensive analyses of inequality and its effects on societies and political systems. Doctoral researchers will benefit from a collaborative atmosphere that encourages intellectual exchange, critical debate, and innovative thinking. Regular seminars, workshops, and academic events provide opportunities for doctoral candidates to present their work, receive feedback from experts, and engage with international scholars working on similar topics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Overview of the Available PhD Positions The Cluster of Excellence is offering seven doctoral positions across several research themes and projects. These positions provide flexibility for candidates to pursue either independent research or collaborative research within specific cluster projects. Two of the positions are independent PhD positions , allowing candidates to design and conduct their own research projects related to the politics of inequality. These positions are ideal for applicants who have a well-developed research idea aligned with the cluster s overarching themes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Another position is located within the Working Group Comparative Political Economy , supervised by Prof. Dr. Marius R. Busemeyer . This research group focuses on the relationships between political institutions, economic systems, and social inequality, offering doctoral researchers the opportunity to examine policy-making processes and institutional responses to inequality. One doctoral position is connected to the project Inequalities in Climate Finance (ClimFin). This research project investigates how financial resources for climate mitigation and adaptation are distributed globally and how these distributions may reinforce or reduce existing inequalities between countries and communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Another position is linked to the project Students Perceptions of Inequality and Fairness. This project focuses on how young people perceive inequality within educational systems and society, exploring how these perceptions influence their attitudes toward fairness, opportunity, and social mobility. Finally, two doctoral positions are available in the research project Mobilizing Climate Inequalities: From Perceptions to Participation (MOCLI). This project examines how perceptions of climate-related inequalities influence political participation and collective action related to climate justice. Each of these research areas contributes to the broader goal of understanding how inequality shapes political behavior, policy decisions, and social change. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Doctoral Training and Graduate School Integration Successful candidates will be enrolled in the Graduate School of the Social and Behavioural Sciences at the University of Konstanz . Within this graduate school, doctoral researchers will specialize in Inequality Research , gaining advanced training in both theoretical and methodological approaches relevant to the study of inequality. The graduate school provides structured doctoral training, including coursework, research seminars, professional development workshops, and opportunities to participate in international conferences. Doctoral candidates will receive guidance from experienced supervisors while also benefiting from peer collaboration within the doctoral community. The academic environment at the University of Konstanz encourages independent research while providing strong institutional support. Doctoral candidates are expected to actively contribute to the intellectual life of the cluster by presenting research findings, participating in collaborative projects, and engaging with scholars from different disciplines. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Application Requirements and Process Applicants interested in one of the seven doctoral positions must submit their applications through the official online application portal of the University of Konstanz . The application must include several important documents that allow the selection committee to assess the candidate s academic background and research potential. Required documents include: A motivation letter explaining the applicant s research interests and reasons for applying A detailed curriculum vitae outlining academic achievements and relevant experience A letter of reference from an academic referee Academic certificates and transcripts demonstrating previous qualifications ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Applicants must clearly indicate which PhD project they are applying for in both the statement of purpose and the electronic application form. Following the initial evaluation process, shortlisted candidates may be asked to submit a three- to four-page research proposal that outlines their proposed doctoral research topic. This proposal should demonstrate how the applicant s research aligns with the themes and objectives of the cluster. Candidates who advance to the final stage of the selection process will be invited to participate in an interview scheduled for the first half of May . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Why Pursue a PhD at the University of Konstanz? The University of Konstanz is internationally recognized for its excellence in research and teaching. As a member of Germany s prestigious Excellence Strategy , the university hosts several high-impact research clusters and offers an inspiring environment for early-career researchers. Doctoral candidates in the Cluster of Excellence The Politics of Inequality will benefit from: Access to a world-class interdisciplinary research community Opportunities to collaborate with leading scholars in the field Participation in innovative research projects addressing global societal challenges Structured doctoral training and professional development Exposure to international academic networks and research collaborations By joining this cluster, doctoral researchers will contribute to a deeper understanding of inequality and help generate knowledge that can inform public policy, political debate, and social change. Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications is 15 April 2026 . Interested applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their application materials early and submit them through the official University of Konstanz application portal before the deadline. For more information click here Discover more opportunities here The post 7 Doctoral Positions at the University of Konstanz: Join the “Politics of Inequality” Research Cluster in Germany appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Agenda for Change Women in WASH Mentorship Program 2026: Free Global Mentorship for Women in Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (Apply by March 20)
summary: Applications are now open for the Agenda for Change Women in WASH Mentorship Program 2026 , a global initiative designed to empower women working in the water, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) sector. Women in the WASH sector often face fewer role models and limited professional networks compared to their male counterparts. This mentorship program aims to address that gap by connecting women professionals with experienced mentors through structured one-on-one guidance. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Organized by Agenda for Change , the program supports career growth, leadership development, and professional networking for women working across the water and sanitation sector. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Mentorship Program The Women in WASH Mentorship Program is a free six-month virtual initiative that runs from April to September 2026 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Participants will engage in: One-on-one mentorship sessions Professional networking opportunities Leadership and career development support Cross-country collaboration among WASH professionals The program is conducted fully online and is accessible globally. New Local Mentorship Cohorts For the first time, the program is also introducing local mentorship cohorts in four countries , strengthening regional support networks for women in the WASH sector. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); These include: Guatemala: In partnership with Universidad de San Carlos de Guatemala through the EPSUM program Ghana: Collaboration with Ghana Water Limited and Coalition of NGOs in Water and Sanitation Ethiopia: Partnering with Drop of Water and its Youth for WASH initiative Cambodia: Working with Center for Sustainable Water These cohorts aim to strengthen local professional networks while maintaining global mentorship opportunities. Program Features Participants will benefit from: One-to-one mentorship from experienced professionals A supportive international network of women in WASH Flexible virtual participation Access to mentorship in English, French, and Spanish Opportunities to share experiences and develop leadership skills ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); The program is designed to support both mentors and mentees , welcoming professionals at different stages of their careers. Additional Mentorship Opportunity Applicants may also apply for a complementary mentorship program offered in collaboration with Rural Water Supply Network . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); This additional program is open to water professionals of all genders and can be accessed through the same application link. Application Deadline Applications close on March 20, 2026 . Early applications are encouraged due to the high level of interest in the program. How to Apply Apply through the official application portal For more OFY mentorship opportunities, Visit Here. The post Agenda for Change Women in WASH Mentorship Program 2026: Free Global Mentorship for Women in Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene (Apply by March 20) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: 2027 Irvine Awards: Nominate Transformative Leaders in California to Receive $350,000 in Support
summary: Do you know a leader or leadership team driving transformative solutions to the most pressing issues facing California? The 2027 Irvine Awards are now open for nominations. Presented by the James Irvine Foundation , the Irvine Awards recognize innovative leaders who are tackling critical challenges in California communities. The program celebrates individuals or leadership pairs whose work demonstrates impactful solutions that can influence public policy and improve lives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Each selected award recipient s organization receives $350,000 in funding , along with strategic support to amplify their ideas and share their solutions with policymakers and community leaders across the state. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Irvine Awards The Irvine Awards are designed to spotlight leaders who are making a measurable difference in California through innovative programs, advocacy, or community-driven initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The award focuses on leaders whose solutions address complex issues such as: Economic opportunity Education and workforce development Community resilience Public policy innovation Social equity and inclusion The program not only recognizes leadership excellence but also helps scale solutions that can benefit communities across California. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Award Benefits Organizations associated with selected leaders receive: $350,000 in funding to support their work Increased visibility for their initiatives Opportunities to engage with policymakers and community leaders Strategic support to expand their solutions and impact This support helps ensure that successful ideas reach broader audiences and influence meaningful change. Who Should Be Nominated? Nominations are encouraged for leaders or leadership pairs who: Are advancing innovative solutions to major challenges in California Demonstrate measurable community impact Lead organizations committed to public good Have ideas or programs with the potential to influence policy and scale across communities ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Nomination Deadline All nominations must be submitted by March 20, 2026 . Late submissions are typically not considered. How to Submit a Nomination Nominate a deserving leader or leadership pair through the official link. title: Africa–Europe Youth Collaboration Programme 2026
summary: Are you a young changemaker passionate about renewable energy, climate action, and sustainable development? The Africa Europe Joint Transition Programme by YES Europe offers a transformative opportunity to collaborate across continents, gain practical research experience, and build a global network all while exploring solutions for the energy transition of tomorrow. What Is the Africa Europe Joint Transition Programme? The Africa Europe Joint Transition Programme is a youth centred, online collaboration initiative that brings together young leaders from Africa and Europe to research and co create solutions on energy transition and sustainability. Participants work in cross continental pairs, combining perspectives, expertise, and creativity to tackle climate and energy related topics. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This programme seeks to: Encourage mutual learning and cross regional understanding of energy transition challenges. Build practical research and collaboration skills. Promote youth driven voices in sustainability dialogues. Strengthen Africa Europe networks for future action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Who Can Apply? Qualifications Eligibility To qualify for the Africa Europe Joint Transition Programme, you must meet the following criteria: 1. Age: 18 35 years old 2. Region: A national or resident of a country in Africa or Europe 3. Profile: Students, young professionals, early career researchers, or emerging sustainability leaders 4. Interest: Passionate about renewable energy, climate solutions, sustainable development, and collaborative research ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Experience: No formal educational or professional experience is required motivation, teamwork spirit, and curiosity matter most. What You ll Do Programme Structure Once selected, you ll: 1. Get Matched with a Research Partner You ll be paired one on one with a peer from the other continent ensuring rich diversity in perspective and thought. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); 2. Receive Expert Mentorship Senior professionals in energy and sustainability provide guidance throughout the project. 3. Collaborate Virtually on a Research Project Choose from topics such as: Renewable energy policy comparisons Off grid energy solutions Community led clean energy success stories Gender and energy access Financing models for sustainable energy systems Digital innovation for climate and energy solutions 4. Participate in Webinars Workshops Gain knowledge from interactive sessions featuring experts, youth leaders, and peers. 5. Present Your Findings Your research will be showcased through publications, online events, and YES Europe s media channels amplifying your work to a global audience. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Key Benefits Why This Programme Matters Being part of this programme offers: International Research Collaboration Experience Build research skills with a cross continental partner. Expert Mentorship Learn directly from thought leaders and sustainability professionals. Cross Continental Network Connect with a vibrant community of young energy leaders and experts. Publication Visibility Your research can be published and shared across global platforms. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Career Leadership Growth Stand out in the sustainability and energy sectors with hands on experience and credentials. Fully Online Format Participate from anywhere no travel or relocation required. Step by Step How to Apply Step 1: Prepare Your Materials Get ready to share: A short personal profile or CV A motivation statement outlining your interest in energy transition Any relevant project ideas or interests (if available) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); You do not need formal certificates passion and potential count! Step 2: Complete the Online Application Apply here Step 3: Await Confirmation Selected applicants will receive pairing and further programme details by email in March 2026. Step 4: Join the Collaborative Journey Engage in webinars, research meetings, and publishing activities throughout the programme timeline. 2026 Programme Timeline Key Deadlines Milestone Deadline Application Opens NOW OPEN Application Deadline 16th March 2026 (may close early if quota fills) Pairing and Selection Late March 2026 Online Collaboration Phase Begins April 2026 Webinars, Workshops Exchanges Begin April May 2026 Final Research Presentation Publication June 2026 Programme Conclusion Certification July 2026 Note: YES Europe encourages early submissions to avoid missing the application window due to high interest. The programme is fully online and flexible around your commitments. The joint transition programme is free of charge for all selected participants. Motivation and collaborative mindset are the most important qualifications. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Africa–Europe Youth Collaboration Programme 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: PhD in Public Governance, Management and Policy Scholarship | University of Bologna
summary: Are you ready to join a prestigious, international doctoral research community focused on governance, public management, and policy innovation? The PhD in Public Governance, Management and Policy (Public-GoMaP) at the University of Bologna (Alma Mater Studiorum) is now accepting applications for the 2026-2027 cycle offering a fully funded pathway to a world-class academic and professional research career in public administration, policy analysis, and governance innovation. Eligibility Qualification Requirements To apply, candidates must generally hold: 1. A Master s degree (or equivalent) in relevant fields such as Public Policy, Political Science, Economics, Management, Statistics or related disciplines. 2. Demonstrated interest in governance, policy analysis, public management, or related research topics. 3. Proficiency in English or Italian (program is taught in both languages). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Specific eligibility checks (e.g. degree equivalence for international applicants) may vary consult the official Call for Applications for detailed enrollment rules. What You ll Study Programme Focus The Public-GoMaP PhD builds interdisciplinary research and analytical capacity across: Public Governance Management Systems Policy Design, Implementation Evaluation Performance Assessment Techniques Digital Transformation in Public Sector Data-Driven Decision Making Ethics and Responsible Governance in Public Administration ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Students work closely with faculty and external stakeholders, producing publishable research and presenting findings internationally. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Scholarship Benefits Fully Funded Scholarship All admitted PhD candidates receive a scholarship of at least: 1,250 net per month for the full duration of the programme (three years). This amount may be adjusted for pension contributions or if the student holds additional income. Additional Advantages Opportunities to collaborate with internationally recognized researchers Active support for presenting work at conferences Access to interdisciplinary research network and resources Training tailored for both academic and professional careers ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); How to Apply Step-by-Step 1. Review the Official Call for Applications Access the Call for Applications link here 2. Prepare Your Documents Ensure the following required documents are prepared: Updated Curriculum Vitae (academic professional) Motivation letter detailing your research interests and goals Academic transcripts and degree certificates Any published work (optional but beneficial) Supporting letters/references (if requested by the selection board) CV in the format specified by the Attachment 1 (CV Summary Form) Note: All documents should be presented as instructed in the official call. Incomplete files may lead to automatic disqualification. 3. Submit Online Applications are usually submitted through the University s official PhD admissions portal. Pay attention to: Confirming submission receipt Ensuring your email is active for further correspondence Meeting all technical instructions on file formats and size limits ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Key Deadlines You Must Know Milestone Deadline Application Deadline (42nd Cycle First Round) 30th March 2026 23:59 CET Doctoral Programme Start Date 1st November 2026 Doctoral Duration 3 years Applicants should ensure submission well before the deadline to avoid technical issues or missing attachments such as CV and supporting documents. Career Impact Opportunities Graduates of the Public-GoMaP PhD can pursue roles such as: Policy Analyst Advisor Public Sector Management Specialist Consultant in Governance Evaluation Project Coordinator at National/International Bodies Academic Researcher ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Positions are available across: Government agencies International organizations Research institutes NGOs consulting firms Academia think tanks For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Apply Now: PhD in Public Governance, Management and Policy Scholarship | University of Bologna appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women’s Leadership in Peace and Security 2026: 3-Day Leadership Program in Geneva
summary: Women leaders play a critical role in advancing peace, stability, and security worldwide. The Women s Leadership in Peace and Security 2026 program offers a powerful opportunity for mid-career women professionals to strengthen their leadership capacity, build meaningful networks, and gain practical tools to lead with confidence in complex global environments. Hosted in Geneva, Switzerland , this immersive three-day in-person program is designed specifically for women working in the peace and security sector. Participants will learn from experienced facilitators, engage with peers across sectors, and explore proven strategies that help women thrive in leadership roles. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Through interactive learning sessions, participants will gain new perspectives, deepen their self-awareness, and develop the skills needed to influence change and navigate uncertainty in today s rapidly evolving global landscape. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Overview The program brings together a vibrant community of women committed to growth, learning, and mutual support. Over three days, participants will explore the seven key ways women can succeed as leaders while strengthening their leadership presence and decision-making skills. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The experience combines expert facilitation, peer learning, and practical leadership exercises that help participants translate insights into real-world impact. Learning Objectives Participants will: Understand the mindsets and behaviors of high-achieving women leaders Deepen self-awareness and leadership identity Gain practical tools and strategies to lead and influence with confidence Improve both personal and team leadership effectiveness Build strong professional networks with peers from across sectors Exchange experiences and perspectives within a supportive cohort ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Who Should Apply This program is designed for: Mid-career women professionals Individuals living in Geneva Professionals working in the peace and security sector Women seeking to strengthen their leadership influence and expand their professional networks Program Benefits Participants will: Expand their leadership perspective and master modern leadership skills Build confidence and self-awareness as leaders Join a mission-driven network of women leaders in peace and security Learn directly from experienced facilitators and sector experts Develop connections that continue long after the program ends ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Program Details Location: Geneva, Switzerland Dates: 30 March 01 April 2026 Duration: 3 Days (In-Person) Program Fee: 1200 CHF Participants based in Geneva who originate from conflict-affected or fragile regions and are unable to pay the tuition fee are encouraged to apply for a tuition waiver . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Please note that the organizers reserve the right to accept or decline applications and may modify program dates, delivery mode, or fees if necessary. Application Deadline 15 March 2026 Early applications are encouraged as places may be limited. How to Apply Interested candidates should submit their application before the deadline through the official application portal. Apply here Discover more Job opportunities by clicking Here. title: Fully Funded Jardine Graduate Scholarships at University of Oxford 2026 – Tuition, Stipend and Airfare Covered
summary: Are you an aspiring Master s or PhD student from Asia dreaming of studying at the University of Oxford? The Jardine Graduate Scholarships 2026 offered through Jardine Graduate Scholarships The Queen s College, Oxford could be your gateway to one of the world s most prestigious universities fully funded, inclusive of living costs, airfare, and more. What Are the Jardine Graduate Scholarships? The Jardine Graduate Scholarships are fully funded postgraduate scholarships for international students from selected Asian countries, designed to support exceptional candidates who demonstrate academic excellence, leadership potential, and a commitment to community service . The scholarships are tenable at partner colleges of Oxford University, including The Queen s College one of the most prestigious and historic colleges in Oxford. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Who Is Eligible? To qualify for the Jardine Graduate Scholarships (via Queen s College): Nationality/Residency: You must be a citizen of or have been resident for at least seven years in one of the following countries/territories: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Cambodia Mainland China Hong Kong SAR Indonesia Malaysia Myanmar The Philippines Singapore Taiwan Thailand Vietnam ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Academic Offer: You must already have an offer to study a graduate course at the University of Oxford before your scholarship application can be considered. Course Type: Available for any full-time Masters or DPhil programme offered at Oxford (subject to college rules). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Scholarship Coverage What You Get The Jardine Graduate Scholarship is highly generous and covers almost all your financial needs while studying in the UK: 1. Full University Tuition Fees Paid directly to Oxford University. 2. College Fees Covered by the scholarship. 3. Annual Stipend For board, lodging, and all incidental expenses, paid in installments. 4. UK Immigration Health Surcharge Included. 5. Return Economy Airfare Reimbursed at cost for travel between your home country and the UK, at the start and end of your studies. 6. Duration: Up to 4 years for DPhil or other extended programmes. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Benefits Beyond Money This scholarship not only funds your studies but connects you to an influential global network of Jardine Scholars, including events, alumni communities, and leadership opportunities that can boost your future career prospects. Step-by-Step Application Process 1. Secure an Offer at Oxford You must first apply for the graduate programme you wish to pursue at Oxford University and receive an offer of admission. Check the official University deadlines for your course. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Oxford course applications are submitted through the University admissions portal. 2. Download and Complete the Scholarship Form Once you have an offer: Download the Jardine Graduate Scholarship application form (linked on the Queen s College webpage). Complete all required sections accurately. 3. Prepare Supporting Documents Essential documents usually include: 1. Academic transcripts 2. Personal statement 3. CV/Resume 4. Reference letter (sent directly from referee) 5. Evidence of residency/nationality criteria ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Reference Letter: Must be sent by your referee from their official email by 13 March 2026 . Late letters will disqualify your application. 4. Submit Your Application Send completed application and attachments to: Admissions and Graduate Administrator admissions@queens.ox.ac.uk Include your Oxford application number in all communications Apply here Key Tips for a Competitive Application Start early deadlines and admissions timelines overlap. Choose a strong referee who knows your academic and leadership potential. Tailor your personal statement to highlight leadership, impact, and how you plan to contribute back home. Ensure document quality: transcripts, language proof (if required), and residency evidence. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Important Deadlines (2026 Intake) Deadline Action Required By 12:00pm GMT, Friday 13 March 2026 Submit completed scholarship application form and supporting documents to Queen s College admissions. By 12:00pm GMT, Friday 13 March 2026 Your reference letter must be received directly from your referee. By Friday 27 March 2026 Oxford offer must be confirmed to Queen s College otherwise application is ineligible. Before applying You need a formal offer of admission from Oxford. Tip: Oxford graduate course deadlines are usually in December/January (depending on your programme). Make sure you apply early to secure an offer in time for the scholarship! The Jardine Graduate Scholarship is more than financial support it s a launchpad for future leaders from Asia to study at one of the world s most elite institutions. It enables talented students to access world-class education without financial barriers, gain global exposure, and build a career that impacts communities in their home countries and beyond. For more information visit here For more global opportunities click here The post Fully Funded Jardine Graduate Scholarships at University of Oxford 2026 – Tuition, Stipend and Airfare Covered appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: Communications Manager (Remote) at Co-Impact
summary: Are you an experienced communications professional passionate about storytelling, global development, and gender equality? The global philanthropic organization Co-Impact is hiring a Communications Manager to strengthen its global voice and amplify impact across health, education, and economic systems. This fully remote, full-time role is open to candidates authorized to work in the United Kingdom or the United States (East Coast) and offers an annual salary of $112,000 plus a generous benefits package . If you have strong editorial skills, experience in development or philanthropy, and a passion for telling impactful stories, this opportunity could be a great fit. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); About Co-Impact Co-Impact is a global collaborative that brings together local changemakers and funders to strengthen health, education, and economic systems. The organization focuses on large-scale systems change , aiming to address the root causes of inequality affecting women, girls, and marginalized communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Working across Africa, Asia, and Latin America , Co-Impact provides large, flexible grants and strategic support to partners leading transformative initiatives in their communities. Through its partnerships and funding model, the organization aims to benefit more than 600 million people and deploy over $1 billion by 2030 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the Communications Manager Role The Communications Manager will play a key role in strengthening Co-Impact s external communications and storytelling. Reporting to the Associate Director of Communications , the role involves developing compelling content, managing communications projects, and supporting media engagement to advance the organization s mission and visibility. This is a hands-on position for a communications professional who can move seamlessly between strategy and execution , producing high-quality content for diverse audiences in a fast-paced global environment. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Key Responsibilities 1. Impact Storytelling and Content Creation Translate strategic priorities into clear, consistent messaging and high-quality content Lead editorial development of key communications materials Create compelling impact stories and maintain the organization s story bank Develop reports, campaign materials, and leadership communications Draft talking points, Q A documents, briefing notes, and background materials Ensure all content aligns with brand guidelines and messaging 2. Media Support and External Positioning Coordinate with media agencies and partners for media outreach Support both proactive and reactive media engagement during major announcements Monitor the media landscape and guide communications strategy ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 3. Communications Planning and Project Management Develop and manage communications plans and timelines Coordinate across teams to gather information and produce content Manage multiple communications projects simultaneously Provide updates and ensure stakeholders remain aligned Why Join Co-Impact? Working with Co-Impact offers the opportunity to: Shape global narratives around systems change and gender equality Work with leading funders and changemakers worldwide Contribute to initiatives benefiting hundreds of millions of people Join a diverse, mission-driven global team ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); If you are passionate about storytelling for social impact and want to support transformative global initiatives, this role offers a meaningful platform to do so. Eligibility Requirements Applicants should meet the following qualifications: Minimum 5 years of experience in communications, media relations, or related fields Strong writing, editing, and storytelling skills Experience producing communications materials under tight deadlines Experience collaborating with designers or creative teams Background in philanthropy, development, or gender equality sectors Experience working with funders or Global South contexts is strongly preferred Strong English communication skills (written and spoken) A university degree in communications, media, or a related field Ability to work across time zones and travel when needed ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Knowledge of gender equality and systems change approaches will be an added advantage. Essential Skills The ideal candidate should demonstrate: Strong project management and organizational skills Strategic thinking and the ability to translate strategy into messaging Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate across cultures Sound judgment and analytical thinking Experience supporting senior leaders and managing stakeholder expectations High integrity, professionalism, and adaptability in global environments Proficiency in Microsoft Word, Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint Salary and Benefits Annual Salary: $112,000 Work Arrangement: Fully Remote Location Eligibility: United Kingdom or United States (East Coast) Benefits: Generous benefits package Co-Impact follows a compensation philosophy based on equity, transparency, and accountability , ensuring fair compensation regardless of gender, race, or nationality. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Application Process Interested candidates should apply here. Your cover letter should also confirm that you are authorized to live and work in the UK or US (East Coast) . Application Deadline: March 30, 2026 (5:00 PM Eastern Time) Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis , so early submission is strongly encouraged. Shortlisted candidates can expect to hear from the recruitment team by the end of April 2026 . Discover more job opportunities by clicking here. The post Apply Now: Communications Manager (Remote) at Co-Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
